title: Implementing Relationships in NoSQL Databases Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on embedding vs. referencing for relationships in NoSQL databases.

questions.question: What is the primary way to model a 1:1 relationship in a NoSQL document database?
options: Embedding the related data directly within the document
Using only primary keys on both documents

questions.question: Which of these describes 'Referencing' in a NoSQL database?
options: Storing only the ID of a related document in another collection
Making a list of all names in the database
Creating a primary key for every record
Saving a document as a JSON file

questions.question: When is it better to use embedding instead of referencing?
options: When data is large and frequently modified
When related data is small, fixed, and always queried with the parent
When a single record belongs to many parents simultaneously
When using a relational database only

questions.question: What process is used in ODMs like Mongoose to replace a reference ID with its actual document content?
